From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Type of undergraduate qualification

"B.A." and "A.B." redirect here. For other uses, see [BA (disambiguation)](/wiki/BA_(disambiguation) "BA (disambiguation)") and [AB (disambiguation)](/wiki/AB_(disambiguation) "AB (disambiguation)").

"BAs" redirects here. For chemical compound, see [boron arsenide](/wiki/Boron_arsenide "Boron arsenide").

For the R. K. Narayan novel, see [The Bachelor of Arts](/wiki/The_Bachelor_of_Arts "The Bachelor of Arts"). For the film, see [Bachelor of Arts (film)](/wiki/Bachelor_of_Arts_(film) "Bachelor of Arts (film)").

Bachelor of Arts

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| A Bachelor of Arts degree from [Northwestern University](/wiki/Northwestern_University "Northwestern University") | |
| Acronym | BA AB |
| Type | [Bachelor's degree](/wiki/Bachelor%27s_degree "Bachelor's degree") |
| Duration | Three or four years, depending on the country |

A **Bachelor of Arts** (abbreviated **BA** or **AB**; from the [Latin](/wiki/Latin "Latin") **baccalaureus artium**, **baccalaureus in artibus**, or **artium baccalaureus**) is the holder of a [bachelor's degree](/wiki/Bachelor%27s_degree "Bachelor's degree") awarded for an [undergraduate](/wiki/Undergraduate_education "Undergraduate education") program in the [liberal arts](/wiki/Liberal_arts_education "Liberal arts education"),[[1]](#cite_note-1) or, in some cases, other disciplines.

A Bachelor of Arts degree course is generally completed in three or four years, depending on the country and institution. [*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

* Degree attainment typically takes five or more years in Argentina, Brazil, Chile and Peru. [*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]
* Degree attainment typically takes four years in Afghanistan, Armenia, Azerbaijan, Bangladesh, Belgium, Brunei, Bulgaria, Canada (except Quebec), China, the Czech Republic, Egypt, Finland, Georgia, Ghana, Greece, Hong Kong, Hungary, Indonesia, Iran, Iraq, Ireland, Jamaica, Japan, Kazakhstan, Kenya, Kuwait, Latvia, Lebanon, Lithuania, Malaysia, Mexico, Mongolia, Myanmar, Nepal, the Netherlands, Nigeria, Pakistan, the Philippines, Qatar, Russia, Saudi Arabia, [Scotland](/wiki/Scotland "Scotland"), Serbia, Singapore, Slovakia, Slovenia, South Africa, South Korea, Spain, Sri Lanka, Sweden, Taiwan, Thailand, Turkey, Ukraine, the United States, and Zambia.[[2]](#cite_note-2)
* Degree attainment typically takes three years in Albania, Algeria, Australia, Austria, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Croatia, Denmark, Estonia, France, Germany, Iceland, India, Israel,[[3]](#cite_note-3) Italy, Montenegro, Malta, New Zealand, Norway, Poland, Portugal, the [Canadian province](/wiki/Provinces_and_territories_of_Canada "Provinces and territories of Canada") of [Quebec](/wiki/Quebec "Quebec"), South Africa (certain degrees), Switzerland, the United Kingdom (except Scotland), and some countries of the [European Union](/wiki/European_Union "European Union"). In Bangladesh, China, Indonesia, Nigeria, Pakistan, and Russia, three-year BA (associates) courses are also available.[[4]](#cite_note-4) A three-year bachelor's degree usually does not qualify the holder for admission to graduate programs in other countries where four-year bachelor's degrees are the standard prerequisite.

## Definition

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Bachelor_of_Arts&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Definition")]

The Bachelor of Arts (BA) degree is an undergraduate postsecondary degree that puts a focus on [liberal arts](/wiki/Liberal_arts_education "Liberal arts education") and studies.[[5]](#cite_note-:03-5) In comparison, a [Bachelor of Science](/wiki/Bachelor_of_Science "Bachelor of Science") (BS) has a greater focus on science, math, and engineering. The Bachelor of Arts degree is a type of [baccalaureate degree](/wiki/Bachelor%27s_degree "Bachelor's degree").[[6]](#cite_note-:5-6)[[7]](#cite_note-:1-7) A Bachelor of Arts degree is usually completed in four years: that is, it requires four years of full-time coursework during term time. However, just as with other degrees, some may require a longer time period. This is due to factors such as the student's ability, motivation, and access to financial assistance to earn the degree. Just like other baccalaureate degrees, a Bachelor of Arts is historically offered only at public and private universities and colleges.[[5]](#cite_note-:03-5)[[8]](#cite_note-:2-8)[[9]](#cite_note-:6-9) A Bachelor of Arts, just like other bachelor's degrees, is an admission requirement for graduate and professional school. Beginning in the 1990s, junior colleges started to confer their own baccalaureate degrees. In addition to the standard BA degrees, there are career-specific Bachelor of Arts degrees, including [Bachelor of Arts in Functional English](/wiki/Functional_English "Functional English"), [Bachelor of Arts in Administration](/wiki/Bachelor_of_Business_Administration "Bachelor of Business Administration"), [Bachelor of Arts in Interdisciplinary Studies](/wiki/Bachelor_of_Independent_Studies "Bachelor of Independent Studies"), and [Regents Bachelor of Arts](/wiki/University_System_of_West_Virginia#Regents_Bachelor_of_Arts "University System of West Virginia").[[7]](#cite_note-:1-7)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Bachelor_of_Arts&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: History")]

The Bachelor of Arts degree has been prominent in academics for centuries. It influenced [universities](/wiki/University "University") to begin focusing on broad topics such as [algebra](/wiki/Algebra "Algebra"), [psychology](/wiki/Psychology "Psychology"), [biology](/wiki/Biology "Biology"), [art](/wiki/Art "Art"), [history](/wiki/History "History"), and [philosophy](/wiki/Philosophy "Philosophy").

This aspect of the BA degree has been consistent in its history. The Bachelor of Arts degree was formed out of the study of [liberal arts](/wiki/Liberal_arts_education "Liberal arts education").[[5]](#cite_note-:03-5)[[6]](#cite_note-:5-6) *Liberal art* is a term that was applied to the study of many branches of learning such as [grammar](/wiki/Grammar "Grammar"), [logic](/wiki/Logic "Logic"), [rhetoric](/wiki/Rhetoric "Rhetoric"), [arithmetic](/wiki/Arithmetic "Arithmetic"), [geometry](/wiki/Geometry "Geometry"), [astronomy](/wiki/Astronomy "Astronomy"), and [music](/wiki/Music "Music").[[6]](#cite_note-:5-6) The study of liberal arts started during the [Middle Ages](/wiki/Middle_Ages "Middle Ages"). During the [Renaissance](/wiki/Renaissance "Renaissance"), the term *liberal* *art* was meant to describe general studies more broadly.[[5]](#cite_note-:03-5)[[6]](#cite_note-:5-6) This definition of liberal studies remains to this day.

In Japan and the United States, Bachelor of Arts degrees were historically given only by public or private institutions and colleges.[[5]](#cite_note-:03-5)[[9]](#cite_note-:6-9)

## Degrees in Europe

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Bachelor_of_Arts&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Degrees in Europe")]

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [icon](/wiki/File:Question_book-new.svg) | This section **does not [cite](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citing_sources "Wikipedia:Citing sources") any [sources](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability "Wikipedia:Verifiability")**. Unsourced material may be challenged and [removed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability#Burden_of_evidence "Wikipedia:Verifiability"). *(November 2022)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

### Germany

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Bachelor_of_Arts&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Germany")]

In Germany, university-level education usually happens in either a *Universität* (plural: *Universitäten*) or a *[Fachhochschule](/wiki/Fachhochschule "Fachhochschule")* (plural: *Fachhochschulen*); both can be referred to as a *Hochschule*, which is the generic term in Germany for all institutions awarding academic degrees. *Fachhochschule* is often translated as "University of Applied Sciences". *Universitäten* place greater emphasis on fundamental science and background in theory, while *Fachhochschulen* are generally designed with a focus on teaching professional skills. Degrees earned at *Universitäten* and *Fachhochschulen* are legally equivalent.

In Germany, the BA course normally lasts between three and three and a half years—six or seven semesters—and the degree is awarded after the student earns between 180 and 210 [ECTS](/wiki/European_Credit_Transfer_and_Accumulation_System "European Credit Transfer and Accumulation System").

### Netherlands

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Bachelor_of_Arts&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Netherlands")]

In the Netherlands, the BA and Master of Arts (MA) degrees were introduced in 2002. Until then, a single program led to the *[doctorandus](/wiki/Doctorandus "Doctorandus")* degree (abbreviated drs.), which comprised the same course load as the bachelor's and master's programs combined. The title *doctorandus* was used in almost all fields of study; other titles were used for legal studies (*meester*, Dutch for master, abbreviated *Mr.*) and engineering (*[ingenieur](/wiki/Ingenieur "Ingenieur")*, abbreviated Ir. for academic masters level or ING. for higher vocational bachelor's level). Those who had already started the *doctorandus* program could, on completing it, opt for the *doctorandus* degree (entitling them to use "drs." in front of their name) or could use the master's degree (post-nominal letters) in accordance with the new standard. When attaining a master level/graduate degree, it is still customary to use either drs. pre-nominally or MA/MSc post-nominally at the discretion of the holder.

### United Kingdom and Ireland

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Bachelor_of_Arts&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: United Kingdom and Ireland")]

Main article: [British undergraduate degree classification](/wiki/British_undergraduate_degree_classification "British undergraduate degree classification")

In the United Kingdom (excluding Scotland) and Ireland, the first degree course normally lasts three years, but nomenclature varies: 19th-century and later universities usually distinguish between arts and sciences subjects by awarding either a BA or BSc degree. However, some older or [ancient universities](/wiki/Ancient_universities "Ancient universities"), such as the [University of Oxford](/wiki/University_of_Oxford "University of Oxford"), [University of Cambridge](/wiki/University_of_Cambridge "University of Cambridge"), and [Trinity College Dublin](/wiki/Trinity_College_Dublin "Trinity College Dublin") traditionally award BAs to undergraduates having completed the final examinations, e.g., Part II [Tripos](/wiki/Tripos "Tripos") (Cambridge), Final Honour Schools (Oxford), Moderator-ship (Dublin), in most subjects including the sciences. Some new [plate glass universities](/wiki/Plate_glass_university "Plate glass university") established in the 1960s, such as [York](/wiki/University_of_York "University of York") and [Lancaster](/wiki/Lancaster_University "Lancaster University"), originally followed the practice of Oxford and Cambridge by awarding BAs in all subjects, but have since changed to awarding BSc degrees in science subjects. At Oxford, Cambridge, and Dublin the degree of [MA](/wiki/Master_of_Arts_(Oxford,_Cambridge_and_Dublin) "Master of Arts (Oxford, Cambridge and Dublin)") can be claimed, usually twenty-one terms after [matriculation](/wiki/Matriculation "Matriculation"), without any further study. For many centuries, the bachelor's degree was an intermediate step and was awarded for much of the work carried out in later times at secondary schools. The names of the final secondary school exams in France and Spain (and of the [International Baccalaureate](/wiki/International_Baccalaureate "International Baccalaureate")) come from this: *le [Baccalauréat](/wiki/Baccalaur%C3%A9at "Baccalauréat")* and *el Bachillerato*, respectively.

The [ancient universities of Scotland](/wiki/Ancient_universities_of_Scotland "Ancient universities of Scotland") award a [Master of Arts](/wiki/Master_of_Arts_(Scotland) "Master of Arts (Scotland)") degree to humanities or arts graduates, but a BSc to science graduates. This course takes four years for an honours degree and three for an ordinary. In Scotland, one can opt to take an ordinary degree, which ranks below a third class honours degree (for example, BA with distinction, merit or pass).

A Bachelor of Arts is entitled to the [post-nominal letters](/wiki/Post-nominal_letters "Post-nominal letters") BA for an ordinary or pass degree and BA (Hons) for an honours degree. The [academic dress](/wiki/Academic_dress "Academic dress") worn by honours and non-honours graduates is identical. An honours degree is always awarded in one of four classes, depending upon the marks gained in the final assessments and examinations. The top students are awarded a first-class degree, followed by an upper second-class degree (usually referred to as a 2:1), a lower second-class degree (usually referred to as a 2:2), and those who pass with the lowest marks gain a third-class degree. An ordinary, pass or unclassified degree (which does not give the graduate the right to add "Hons") may be awarded if a student has completed (i) followed a non-honours program, or (b) attempted an honours course but marginally failed to achieve third-class standard.

## Degrees in North America

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Bachelor_of_Arts&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Degrees in North America")]

### Canada

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Bachelor_of_Arts&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Canada")]

Education in Canada is controlled by the [provinces](/wiki/Canadian_provinces "Canadian provinces") and can be very different depending on the province. While all Canadian universities offer four-year degrees, it is not uncommon, depending on the province and the university for a three-year general degree to also be offered as an option. In many universities and colleges, Bachelor of Arts degrees are differentiated either as BA or as honours BA degrees. Honours programs require more education than non-honours programs, typically a specialization beyond the requirements of a BA, and can often be used as a gateway to a PhD program, bypassing a master's degree.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

In [Québec](/wiki/Qu%C3%A9bec "Québec"), BA degrees generally last three years, because the fourth year is replaced by [CEGEP](/wiki/CEGEP "CEGEP") education (which fills the gap between Secondary V, the last year of high school, and the first year of university).[[10]](#cite_note-10) However, four-year programs exist for students having completed 12 (instead of 13) years of education prior to being admitted to university.[[11]](#cite_note-11)

### United States

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Bachelor_of_Arts&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: United States")]

Along with the [Bachelor of Science](/wiki/Bachelor_of_Science "Bachelor of Science") (BS), the Bachelor of Arts (BA) is the most commonly granted degree in the United States. A BA degree is usually earned after the completion of four years of [undergraduate](/wiki/Undergraduate "Undergraduate") college level study.[[5]](#cite_note-:03-5)

Some universities in the United States abbreviate the degree as "AB" or "A.B.", derived from the Latin title *Artium Baccalaureatus*. AB degrees are particularly associated with [Ivy League](/wiki/Ivy_League "Ivy League") institutions and other universities with historical ties to Latin academic traditions—such as [Harvard](/wiki/Harvard_University "Harvard University"), [Princeton](/wiki/Princeton_University "Princeton University"), [Brown](/wiki/Brown_University "Brown University"), [UChicago](/wiki/University_of_Chicago "University of Chicago"), and [Bryn Mawr College](/wiki/Bryn_Mawr_College "Bryn Mawr College"). These schools use the AB (or A.B.) designation to distinguish their degree from the more common Bachelor of Arts (BA). The use of Latin terminology underscores their heritage and the prestige of their academic programs.[[12]](#cite_note-12) Additionally, the study of Greek and Latin has traditionally been a fundamental component of the undergraduate curriculum at these institutions.[[13]](#cite_note-13) The [College of Charleston](/wiki/College_of_Charleston "College of Charleston") also offers the A.B. degree as an option.[[14]](#cite_note-14)

## Degrees in other countries

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Bachelor_of_Arts&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Degrees in other countries")]

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [icon](/wiki/File:Question_book-new.svg) | This section **needs additional citations for [verification](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability "Wikipedia:Verifiability")**. Please help [improve this article](/wiki/Special:EditPage/Bachelor_of_Arts "Special:EditPage/Bachelor of Arts") by [adding citations to reliable sources](/wiki/Help:Referencing_for_beginners "Help:Referencing for beginners") in this section. Unsourced material may be challenged and removed. *Find sources:* ["Bachelor of Arts"](https://www.google.com/search?as_eq=wikipedia&q=%22Bachelor+of+Arts%22) – [news](https://www.google.com/search?tbm=nws&q=%22Bachelor+of+Arts%22+-wikipedia&tbs=ar:1) **·** [newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22Bachelor+of+Arts%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22Bachelor+of+Arts%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22Bachelor+of+Arts%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22Bachelor+of+Arts%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(April 2025)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

In colleges and universities in Australia, Nepal, New Zealand, India and South Africa, the BA degree can be taken over three years of full-time study.[[15]](#cite_note-15) Students must pursue at least one [major](/wiki/Academic_major "Academic major") area of study and units from that subject are usually studied in each year, though sometimes students may choose to complete upper-level classes in the same year and as a result, can leave space for elective subjects from a different field. At some universities, students may choose to pursue a second major; alternatively, the remainder of the degree is taken up with a minor area of study (in the first two years) and other individual or stream-based subjects.
In Pakistan the Bachelor of Arts (BA) program used to be regular 2-year degree program in which students were offered with a combination of any two of areas from the available electives: Business, Journalism, Psychology, Computer Science and Education. However, now all bachelor's degree courses are 4-year program. 2 years degree is now called associate degree.
